title: UnitAndValuePreferences Object
description: The UnitAndValuePreferences object provides access to unit and value precision related preferences.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: angularPrecision
properties.description: Gets and sets the angular precision. This value specifies the number of decimals to display.

properties.name: areAbbreviationsForUnitDisplayed
properties.description: Gets and sets if abbreviations are used for units display.

properties.name: areSymbolsForUnitDisplayed
properties.description: Gets and sets if symbols are used for units display.

properties.name: areTrailingZerosHidden
properties.description: Gets and sets if trailing zeros are hidden when displaying numbers.

properties.name: degreeDisplayFormat
properties.description: Gets and sets the degree display format.

properties.name: footAndInchDisplayFormat
properties.description: Gets and sets the foot and inch display format.

properties.name: generalPrecision
properties.description: Gets and sets the general precision for distance values. This value specifies the number of decimals to display.

properties.name: isPeriodDecimalPoint
properties.description: Gets and sets if the decimal is a period or comma.

properties.name: isScientificNotationUsed
properties.description: Gets and sets if scientific notation is used when displaying numbers.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: materialDisplayUnit
properties.description: Gets and sets the units types to use when displaying values.

properties.name: minimumPrecisionWhenHidingZeros
properties.description: Gets and sets the minimum number of digits to the right of the decimal to display before hiding trailing zeros.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: scientificNotationPrecision
properties.description: Gets and sets the number scientific notation precision. This value specifies the number of decimals to display.

properties.name: useScientficNotationAbove
properties.description: Gets and sets the number of whole digits that will be displayed before switching to scientific notation.

properties.name: useScientficNotationBelow
properties.description: Gets and sets the number of non zero decimal places that will be displayed before switching to scientific notation.

angularPrecision.title: UnitAndValuePreferences.angularPrecision Property
angularPrecision.description: Gets and sets the angular precision. This value specifies the number of decimals to display.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = unitAndValuePreferences_var.angularPrecision

# Set the value of the property.
unitAndValuePreferences_var.angularPrecision = propertyValue

classType.title: UnitAndValuePreferences.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.
